methods.description: Process for growing maize accessions in a controlled indoor environment to manage daylength requirements and promote flowering.

steps.description: Seeds were initially grown under growth room conditions in 5×5 inch biodegradable pots filled with a mixture of Sunshine Mix and field soil.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare growth room environment with specific soil mixture for maize seeds

steps.description: A 14/10-h day-night period was adjusted until the seedlings reached the V5 growth stage.
steps.searchable_summary: set day-night cycle to 14/10 hours for seedling growth

steps.description: The photoperiod was reduced to 10 h until the V8 growth stage.
steps.searchable_summary: reduce photoperiod to 10 hours for further growth

steps.description: Fluorescent and LED lights were used to achieve a light intensity of 425-515 μmol m -2 s -1 at pot level.
steps.searchable_summary: set light intensity using fluorescent and LED lights
outputs: light intensity of 425-515 μmol m -2 s -1

steps.description: Pots were moved around twice per week to promote uniform distribution of light.
steps.searchable_summary: rotate pots for uniform light distribution

steps.description: Irrigation and fertilization were done manually using 20:20:20 fertilizer with micronutrients.

methods.description: Procedure for transitioning maize seedlings from indoor growth conditions to field conditions to ensure acclimatization.

steps.description: At the V8 growth stage, 780 large maize seedlings were transported to the field at the Elora Research Station.
Seedlings were left in the field for 3 days (2-3 h/day under direct sunlight and remaining time under shade) in a trailer.
steps.searchable_summary: acclimatize seedlings with controlled sunlight exposure
